input.topic: One-page intake form. 8 minutes. The 14-day clock pauses until it's in.
input.scenario: Sent 60s after deposit-received, or as a nudge if the intake is still empty 24h in. The customer needs to complete an 8-minute form before the build clock can resume.
input.voice: Operator. Names the consequence ('clock pauses') instead of nagging. No 'please' / 'kindly'. Signed '— Jack / Day14'.

variants.subject: Your intake form — 8 minutes, one page
variants.rationale: Keeps the time-cost in the subject ('8 minutes') so the customer doesn't dismiss it as a long form. 'One page' reinforces the low-friction promise.

variants.subject: One page, 8 minutes — then the clock starts
variants.rationale: Leads with the friction-relief, ends on the consequence. The 'then' structure makes the cause-and-effect explicit without scolding.

variants.subject: Intake link inside — clock paused until I have it
variants.rationale: Names the SOW reality up front ('clock paused'). 'Intake link inside' tells the customer exactly what action the email is asking for.
